title: Test-file bloat in tools/mod.rs — 800 lines of tests mixed with production code, reaching into private file/notebook internals
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension:** Testability / Structural Modularity

**Location:** /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/tools/mod.rs lines 1092-1920 (cfg(test) module)

**Violation:**
The test module begins at line 1092 and runs through 1920 — 828 lines, 43% of the file. It imports file::{detect_file_type, parse_page_range, read_image_file, read_notebook_file, source_to_line_array, FileType, READ_TRACKER} (lines 1097-1100), reaching into sibling-module internals. This tangles four concerns:
1. **Private-surface leakage.** read_notebook_file, detect_file_type, parse_page_range, source_to_line_array, FileType, READ_TRACKER are exposed as pub only to satisfy these tests (see file/mod.rs:10-15 which annotate them with #[allow(unused_imports)] 'used by tests in tools::mod'). That annotation is a smell — the tests should be LOCAL to their modules.
2. **SRP violation for the test file itself.** One test module contains tests for: bash, list_files, accumulator (tool-call + Anthropic), file-type detection, page-range parsing, notebook formatting, notebook read, notebook edit (5 scenarios), image read, task manager (7 tests), permission manager (1 test). Each target warrants a co-located #[cfg(test)] in its own module.
3. **Evidence of the pattern spreading.** File/mod.rs lines 9 and 11: 'pub use notebook::{execute_notebook_edit, source_to_line_array}; #[allow(unused_imports)] // used by tests in tools::mod' — production export widened just for tests.

**Mandated Refactor:**
1. Move each test to its relevant module: test_detect_file_type* -> file/read.rs; test_notebook_edit* -> file/notebook.rs; test_bash_execution -> bash/mod.rs; test_list_files -> file/list.rs; test_task_* -> task.rs; test_anthropic_accumulator_* -> accumulator.rs; test_check_tool_permission_* -> permissions module.
2. Revert the widened pub visibility on source_to_line_array, read_notebook_file, READ_TRACKER, etc. — restore to pub(crate) or pub(super).
3. Delete the #[allow(unused_imports)] annotations.
4. Leave only tool-dispatch-level integration tests in tools/mod.rs.
